knownMismatchBuckets.note: Transcript text drift after early alignment.
Speaker attribution drift after transcript shift.
Speaker registry drift tracks attribution drift.
Segment start offsets drift after alignment loss.
Speaker profile descriptors diverge with speaker drift.
Trait labels drift alongside misgrouped profiles.
Missing tail segments because output produced 23 segments while truth expects 30.
Linked profile structure diverges after transcript/speaker drift; truth length 14 vs output length 17.

sessions.summary: Initialized reusable lane config and durable ledger contract using the existing baseline artifacts; no scored optimization attempt executed in this session.

Ran two narrow dialogue optimization attempts, rejected one after it exposed a fallback transport bug, then landed a generic MP3 MIME normalization fix that completed the lane but regressed benchmark accuracy/coverage. Stopped early to avoid thrash.

attempts.proposal: Reject full-span bracketed no-dialogue placeholders in chunked dialogue output so the target chain falls through instead of locking in false negatives.

challengeReview.benchmarkEvidence: Baseline drift includes a long false no-dialogue/music span and a missing lyric/promo tail.
challengeReview.genericityCheck: Applies to generic chunked dialogue pipelines that receive stage-direction placeholders instead of audible words.
challengeReview.regressionRisk: Medium: promoting failover can expose target-chain incompatibilities or alternate-model drift.
challengeReview.scopeCheck: Narrow: one acceptance-rule change in get-dialogue plus one regression test.
challengeReview.successSignal: Same narrow lane completes and improves accuracy without fixture-specific hints.

commands: node scripts/benchmark-iteration-runner.cjs scaffold --lane benchmarks/iterations/lanes/dialogue-gold-optimization.json --session-id 2026-03-30-dialogue-opt-loop --proposal "Reject full-span bracketed no-dialogue placeholders for chunked dialogue transcription so the target chain falls through instead of locking in false negatives." --json
attempts.tradeoffs: Change looked generic, but the run immediately revealed a separate transport compatibility bug in the fallback chain, so the attempt could not be fairly scored and was reverted rather than compounded.
attempts.nextHypothesis: Fix MP3 attachment MIME/format normalization first so fallback-capable dialogue attempts can actually execute.

attempts.proposal: Normalize MP3 dialogue attachments to audio/mp3 so OpenRouter/OpenAI audio fallbacks can execute instead of fatally rejecting input_audio.format=mpeg.

challengeReview.benchmarkEvidence: Attempt 1 exposed a hard 400 from the dialogue target chain that blocked fair scoring.
challengeReview.genericityCheck: Applies to any MP3-backed dialogue/music extraction path using OpenRouter/OpenAI audio input handling.
challengeReview.regressionRisk: Medium: transport normalization can alter which downstream target answers and therefore shift benchmark behavior.
challengeReview.scopeCheck: Narrow: one MIME mapping change plus focused test expectation updates.
challengeReview.successSignal: Focused tests pass, the narrow lane completes, and benchmark accuracy/coverage hold or improve versus baseline.

commands: node scripts/benchmark-iteration-runner.cjs scaffold --lane benchmarks/iterations/lanes/dialogue-gold-optimization.json --session-id 2026-03-30-dialogue-opt-loop --proposal "Normalize MP3 dialogue attachments to audio/mp3 so OpenRouter/OpenAI audio fallbacks can execute instead of fatally rejecting input_audio.format=mpeg." --json
attempts.tradeoffs: Generic fallback compatibility improved, but the completed run produced only 21 dialogue segments versus the truth's 30 and widened structural tail/profile drift.
attempts.baselineDecision: Did not beat baseline; keep baseline-2026-03-30-dialogue-only as active baseline.
attempts.nextHypothesis: Reduce rolling-handoff transcript leakage so later chunks lean on acoustic speaker registry cues without inheriting too much lexical momentum from previous chunks.

notes: This ledger intentionally separates durable lane state from the human-readable plan so future runner tooling can append attempts without scraping markdown.
The current baseline remains the Task 1 dialogue-only run until a future accepted attempt beats or supersedes it.
2026-03-30 session stop note: paused after one unscored blocker-reveal and one scored regression instead of exhausting the five-attempt cap without a stronger next hypothesis.
